claim.content: Let $m^{*}$ denote the quasiparticle effective mass at the Fermi level of a Fermi-liquid state, defined operationally via the low-temperature specific heat coefficient $\gamma$ as $\gamma\propto m^{*}$ (so $\gamma=C/T$ as $T\to0$), and let $T_{\rm coh}$ denote the temperature below which Fermi-liquid thermodynamics (linear specific heat and temperature-independent susceptibilities) are observed. The proposition is that, in a conventional Fermi liquid of itinerant quasiparticles, $T_{\rm coh}$ is proportional to an inverse effective-mass scale, i.e., $T_{\rm coh}\propto 1/m^{*}$ up to factors set by band parameters and quasiparticle residue, which is the standard phenomenological relation between effective mass and the energy window of coherent quasiparticle behavior.
